message:
perf(net,service): consolidate io_driver rearm into single lock and coalesce rate limiter lock acquisitions

TCP and Unix stream split (net/tcp/split.rs, net/unix/split.rs):
Previously, re-arming a stream registration required two separate
io_driver lock acquisitions: set_interest() to re-arm epoll readiness,
then update_waker() to refresh the waker slot. This commit replaces
both calls with a single reg.rearm(interest, &waker) that performs
both operations under one io_driver lock.

The rearm() call returns Ok(true) on success, Ok(false) if the
registration was invalidated (triggering cleanup), or Err for I/O
failures. The waker is now computed before calling rearm() so it can
be passed as a parameter, and the NotConnected error handling is
integrated into a match arm rather than a separate if-let chain.

The same pattern is applied to the half-drop path in TCP split,
replacing the set_interest() + update_waker() sequence with a single
rearm() call guarded by matches!().

Rate limiter (service/rate_limit.rs):
poll_ready_with_time() and Service::poll_ready() previously called
self.refill(now) (acquires lock, refills, drops lock) followed by
self.try_acquire() (acquires lock again, decrements token, drops lock).
This double-lock pattern creates a TOCTOU window where another caller
can steal the token between refill and acquire.

This commit introduces refill_state(&mut state, now) which takes an
already-locked &mut RateLimitState, and inlines both refill + acquire
into a single lock acquisition in poll_ready_with_time() and
Service::poll_ready(). The standalone refill() method is preserved for
callers that only need time-advancement without acquisition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
